name: greater_is_lt
reason: If Greater were classified as Less, the ordering comparison used throughout StrictlyOrderedMap and DelegationMap would be fundamentally broken

name: new_spec_is_end
reason: A KeyIterator wrapping Some(k) should never report as end; if it did, get() would be unreachable and DelegationMap range operations would silently skip all keys

name: above_self
reason: A key should not be strictly above itself in its own iterator; this would break the greatest-lower-bound logic used by DelegationMap::get_internal

name: get_roundtrip_broken
code:
proof fn phi_4_get_roundtrip_broken<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(ki: KeyIterator<K>)
    requires
        !ki.is_end_spec(),
    ensures
        KeyIterator::new_spec(*ki.get_spec()) != ki,
{
}
reason: Extracting a key via get and re-wrapping with new_spec must roundtrip to the same iterator; failure would break between/gap reasoning in DelegationMap::set

name: set_misses_interior
reason: A key strictly between lo and hi must be inside the between range; otherwise DelegationMap::set would fail to update interior keys in the assigned range
